rationale: All six round-1 completeness asks are genuinely closed. ADR-0039 is Accepted; PR #159 fixes the named live path docs while preserving resolver-tier and historical references; release notes are present, opt-in/non-breaking, avoid crash-resume overclaiming, and .goreleaser excludes ^Merge. PR #160 fixes sc0w, 3jq7, and crash-resume ordering with targeted tests, and adds meaningful canonical plus legacy read/write downstream-compat coverage. The committed .beads JSONL and local bd output show spec-106 epic plus .6 reclosed and mindspec-wu7t filed as an open P1. I did not run go test per the read-only instruction, and gh pr diff failed due network, so evidence came from origin branch diffs and git show. Remaining gaps are doc polish, not tag blockers.

findings.issue: RELEASE-NOTES-v0.10.0.md does not explicitly call out the mixed-layout hard-error sharp edge, and does not mention the new --force / --allow-branch migrate-layout escape flags. The flags are present in Cobra flag help, so this is not a tag blocker.

.mindspec/domains/context-system/overview.md still names docs/context-map.md as the Context Map location. This is outside the round-1 named user-facing docs patch and appears to be a stale domain overview line, not a blocker.
git diff --check reports trailing whitespace in .mindspec/specs/106-layout-flatten/reviews/AUTOPILOT-LOG.md. It is review artifact whitespace only and does not block the tag.
